notes_added: Publish capability attestations that bind public claims to private commit and artifact digests without publishing private contents.
Treat participation as present when executable public routes preserve the ability to verify, extend, challenge, or reconnect with the private work.
notes_challenged: A public repository proves private value merely by announcing that private work exists.

agreements: Public evidence must remain executable and contestable rather than promotional.
Private content stays private while public claims bind to stable opaque anchors.
Public proof establishes capability and continuity, not exhaustive semantic value.
disagreements: The conversational reader treats latent participation as part of value; the semantic reader accepts it only when a concrete access, verification, or extension route remains available.
omissions_detected: No complete private corpus was available for beginning-to-end analysis.
No independent cryptographic witness currently counters unilateral rewriting by the repository owner.
revisions_after_discussion: The conversational reader added falsification as a condition for credible invitation.
The semantic reader added preserved future participation as a legitimate capability outcome rather than dismissing it as unverifiable sentiment.

privacy.review: Contains only architecture abstractions, synthetic reader views, opaque digests, test outcomes, and privacy-boundary statements; no private transcript text or personal facts.

background.attention_bias: continuity of participation, custody, interpretive access, and what public traces let future people enter
background.counter_bias: do not equate visibility with value or secrecy with proof
background.experience: preserving valuable collections whose protected contents cannot all be publicly displayed

observations: Public proof preserves the existence and evolution of private work without copying it.
Participation remains present as a real future route when public interfaces, tests, and claims stay executable.
readers.thesis: The public repository proves the private repository matters when it exposes durable invitations to participate: capability claims, tests, validators, and continuity anchors that remain meaningful without revealing protected material.
uncertainties: The complete authorized private corpus and all prior private reader journals were unavailable.

background.attention_bias: claim-evidence bindings, tamper detection, falsification routes, authority boundaries, and false assurance
background.counter_bias: do not reduce experiential or symbolic value to only machine-verifiable metrics
background.experience: testing whether private records support public claims without disclosing protected ledgers

observations: Opaque commit and artifact digests establish stable reference, not semantic truth.
Each public capability claim requires named evidence and a reproducible falsification action.
readers.thesis: A public repository cannot prove hidden meaning directly; it can prove that named capabilities bind to immutable private anchors and passing evidence under an explicit privacy boundary.
uncertainties: No independent witness signature or transparency-log inclusion proof exists yet.
